steps.title: Identify the mechanism that needs support
steps.content: Vega and Price claim NFM may reduce voting probability through an indirect effect. "Indirect" means NFM doesn't affect voting directly — it must first weaken some intermediate variable (political knowledge or interest), and that weakened variable then reduces the likelihood of voting. A supporting finding must establish both links of this chain.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice against the two-link chain: NFM $\Rightarrow$ weakens political knowledge/interest $\Rightarrow$ reduces voting:
  • Choice A — ‘NFM attitude tends to increase in strength as major elections approach, and people are significantly more likely to vote in major elections than in minor elections.’: this is about election type and timing — it doesn't address how NFM affects political knowledge/interest or voting probability through those variables. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘NFM attitude has a strong negative effect on political knowledge and interest, and there is known to be a strong positive correlation between political knowledge and interest and the likelihood of voting.’: this establishes both links — NFM weakens the intermediate variables, and those variables drive voting. Exactly the indirect pathway described. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Political interest is known to have a strong positive effect on likelihood of voting but shows only a weak positive effect on political knowledge, and NFM attitude shows little correlation with either political knowledge or political interest.’: this breaks the first link of the chain, undermining rather than supporting the researchers' claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The likelihood of voting increases as political knowledge increases, and the relationship between NFM attitude and political knowledge tends to strengthen as the size of people's social networks increases.’: this introduces social network size as a confound and doesn't cleanly establish the indirect pathway; the first link (NFM $\Rightarrow$ knowledge) is conditional and unclear. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
